doc: Data the Lab can feed to a strategy TODAY. AVAILABLE means the compiler/executor can wire it into a backtest or live run with no further connector work. PARTIAL means a research path exists but production wiring is missing or scope is tight. PLANNED means we intend to build it; UNAVAILABLE means there is no connector and no near-term plan. Codex owns updates as new connectors come online; bump catalog_version on every change.

capabilities.notes: Daily + intraday bars via Alpaca. Wired into the active strategy bank and the Lab pipeline. The default price feed for equities-sleeve research. Covers any signal derivable from OHLCV bars: open/high/low/close, volume, average daily volume (ADV), trailing returns, momentum, moving averages, RSI, realized volatility, mean reversion bands. Strategies should list this capability_id once for the underlying feed and not emit each derived metric as a separate data requirement.

BTC and ETH only via Alpaca. ~1.5 years of history — sufficient for execution and recent-regime work, insufficient for multi-era analysis. Use ccxt_binance_crypto_ohlcv for longer history (research-only, no execution coupling).
Public Binance OHLCV pulled via ccxt for research backtests only. NOT wired into the executor — execution stays on Alpaca. Operator-pulled: no live connector, scripted backfills.
Live chain via Alpaca. NOT historical: no point-in-time reconstruction for backtests. Strategies that depend on historical chain state need a separate provider before they can be Lab-tested.
Would require historical chain history we don't have. No connector and no construction pipeline. Strategies depending on IV surface skew/term structure should BLOCK.

Wikimedia REST API is freely accessible; research backfill scripts exist. No production connector wired into the Lab pipeline yet — operator-driven pulls. Don't claim AVAILABLE until a daily-update connector lands.
GDELT API is free; backfill scripts exist. No daily-update connector wired into the Lab. Same status caveat as wikipedia_pageviews.
No wired connector. Sample-rate noise + rate limiting on the public endpoint make it a v2-or-later candidate.

Top-100 retail-attention rank with mention/upvote ratios. Wired as the canonical retail-sentiment feed for stocks. THIS IS THE PROXY for r/wallstreetbets, X/Twitter trending tickers, StockTwits, Reddit investing subs, and adjacent retail communities — Ape Wisdom aggregates these into one feed. If a thesis mentions 'WSB / Reddit / X / Twitter / StockTwits / retail buzz / social trending' as a signal, list apewisdom_top100 once and do NOT emit those underlying source names as separate requirements. Raw per-thread velocity is not exposed; only the aggregate rank + mention ratio.
Raw r/wallstreetbets per-thread velocity is NOT pulled separately. Use apewisdom_top100 for the aggregated signal. Strategies that genuinely require sub-Ape-Wisdom granularity should BLOCK.
Market cap + average daily volume reachable via Alpaca asset metadata. Not point-in-time. Strategies needing historical fundamentals (P/E, EPS surprise, balance-sheet items) are NOT covered.
No on-chain connector (Glassnode, Dune, etc.). Strategies referencing whale flows, exchange reserves, miner behavior, or staking metrics should BLOCK.

FRED series are operator-curated as needed for one-off research; no Lab-wired connector. Strategies depending on macro inputs (fed funds path, CPI surprises, yield curve shape) should BLOCK until a connector lands.
